metadata.name: py2vega
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

modules.name: py2vega.functions.object
documentation: Object module that implements mocking Vega object functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.object.merge
documentation:
Merge the input objects object1, object2, etc into a new output object.

Inputs are visited in sequential order, such that key values from later arguments
can overwrite those from earlier arguments. Example: merge({a:1, b:2}, {a:3}) -> {a:3, b:2}.

modules.name: py2vega.functions.type_checking
documentation: Module that implements mocking Vega type checking functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_checking.isArray
documentation: Return true if value is an array, false otherwise.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_checking.isBoolean
documentation: Return true if value is a boolean (true or false), false otherwise.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_checking.isDate
documentation:
Return true if value is a Date object, false otherwise.

This method will return false for timestamp numbers or date-formatted strings; it recognizes Date objects only.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_checking.isDefined
documentation:
Return true if value is a defined value, false if value equals undefined.

This method will return true for null and NaN values.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_checking.isNumber
documentation: Return true if value is a number, false otherwise. NaN and Infinity are considered numbers.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_checking.isObject
documentation: Return true if value is an object (including arrays and Dates), false otherwise.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_checking.isRegExp
documentation: Return true if value is a RegExp (regular expression) object, false otherwise.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_checking.isString
documentation: Return true if value is a string, false otherwise.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_checking.isValid
documentation: Return true if value is not null, undefined, or NaN, false otherwise.

modules.name: py2vega.functions.string
documentation: String module that implements mocking Vega string functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.indexof
documentation: Return the first index of substring in the input string.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.lastindexof
documentation: Return the last index of substring in the input string.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.length
documentation: Return the length of the input string.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.lower
documentation: Transform string to lower-case letters.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.pad
documentation:
Pad a string value with repeated instances of a character up to a specified length.

If character is not specified, a space (‘ ‘) is used. By default, padding is added to the end of a string.
An optional align parameter specifies if padding should be added to the 'left' (beginning), 'center',
or 'right' (end) of the input string.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.parseFloat
documentation: Parse the input string to a floating-point value. Same as JavaScript’s parseFloat.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.parseInt
documentation: Parse the input string to an integer value. Same as JavaScript’s parseInt.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.replace
documentation:
Return a new string with some or all matches of pattern replaced by a replacement string.

The pattern can be a string or a regular expression. If pattern is a string, only the first instance will
be replaced. Same as JavaScript’s String.replace.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.slice
documentation:
Return a section of string between the start and end indices.

If the end argument is negative, it is treated as an offset from the end of the string (length(string) + end).

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.split
documentation:
Return an array of tokens created by splitting the input string according to a provided separator pattern.

The result can optionally be constrained to return at most limit tokens.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.substring
documentation: Return a section of string between the start and end indices.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.trim
documentation: Return a trimmed string with preceding and trailing whitespace removed.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.truncate
documentation:
Truncate an input string to a target length.

The optional align argument indicates what part of the string should be truncated: 'left' (the beginning),
'center', or 'right' (the end). By default, the 'right' end of the string is truncated. The optional
ellipsis argument indicates the string to use to indicate truncated content; by default the ellipsis
character … (…) is used.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.string.upper
documentation: Transform string to upper-case letters.

modules.name: py2vega.functions.formatting
documentation: Formatting module that implements mocking Vega formatting functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.formatting.dayFormat
documentation:
Format a (0-6) weekday number as a full week day name, according to the current locale.

For example: dayFormat(0) -> "Sunday".

functions.name: py2vega.functions.formatting.dayAbbrevFormat
documentation:
Format a (0-6) weekday number as an abbreviated week day name, according to the current locale.

For example: dayAbbrevFormat(0) -> "Sun".

functions.name: py2vega.functions.formatting.format
documentation:
Format a numeric value as a string.

The specifier must be a valid d3-format specifier (e.g., format(value, ',.2f').

functions.name: py2vega.functions.formatting.monthFormat
documentation:
Format a (zero-based) month number as a full month name, according to the current locale.

For example: monthFormat(0) -> "January".

functions.name: py2vega.functions.formatting.monthAbbrevFormat
documentation:
Format a (zero-based) month number as an abbreviated month name, according to the current locale.

For example: monthAbbrevFormat(0) -> "Jan".

functions.name: py2vega.functions.formatting.timeFormat
documentation:
Format a datetime value (either a Date object or timestamp) as a string, according to the local time.

The specifier must be a valid d3-time-format specifier. For example: timeFormat(timestamp, '%A').

functions.name: py2vega.functions.formatting.timeParse
documentation:
Parse a string value to a Date object, according to the local time.

The specifier must be a valid d3-time-format specifier. For example: timeParse('June 30, 2015', '%B %d, %Y').

functions.name: py2vega.functions.formatting.utcFormat
documentation:
Format a datetime value (either a Date object or timestamp) as a string, according to UTC time.

The specifier must be a valid d3-time-format specifier. For example: utcFormat(timestamp, '%A').

functions.name: py2vega.functions.formatting.utcParse
documentation:
Parse a string value to a Date object, according to UTC time.

The specifier must be a valid d3-time-format specifier. For example: utcParse('June 30, 2015', '%B %d, %Y').

modules.name: py2vega.functions.scale
documentation: Scale module that implements mocking Vega scale functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.scale
documentation:
Applie the named scale transform (or projection) to the specified value.

The optional group argument takes a scenegraph group mark item to indicate the
specific scope in which to look up the scale or projection.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.invert
documentation:
Invert the named scale transform (or projection) for the specified value.

The optional group argument takes a scenegraph group mark item to indicate the
specific scope in which to look up the scale or projection.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.copy
documentation:
Return a copy (a new cloned instance) of the named scale transform of projection,
or undefined if no scale or projection is found.

The optional group argument takes a scenegraph group mark item to indicate the
specific scope in which to look up the scale or projection.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.domain
documentation:
Return the scale domain array for the named scale transform, or an empty array
if the scale is not found.

The optional group argument takes a scenegraph group mark item to indicate the
specific scope in which to look up the scale.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.range
documentation:
Return the scale range array for the named scale transform, or an empty array
if the scale is not found.

The optional group argument takes a scenegraph group mark item to indicate the
specific scope in which to look up the scale.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.bandwidth
documentation:
Return the current band width for the named band scale transform, or zero if
the scale is not found or is not a band scale.

The optional group argument takes a scenegraph group mark item to indicate the
specific scope in which to look up the scale.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.bandspace
documentation:
Return the number of steps needed within a band scale, based on the count
of domain elements and the inner and outer padding values.

While normally calculated within the scale itself, this function can be
helpful for determining the size of a chart’s layout.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.gradient
documentation:
Return a linear color gradient for the scale (whose range must be a
continuous color scheme) and starting and ending points p0 and p1,
each an [x, y] array.

The points p0 and p1 should be expressed in normalized coordinates in the
domain [0, 1], relative to the bounds of the item being colored.
If unspecified, p0 defaults to [0, 0] and p1 defaults to [1, 0], for a
horizontal gradient that spans the full bounds of an item. The optional
count argument indicates a desired target number of sample points to take
from the color scale.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.panLinear
documentation:
Given a linear scale domain array with numeric or datetime values, returns a new
two-element domain array that is the result of panning the domain by a fractional delta.

The delta value represents fractional units of the scale range; for example, 0.5
indicates panning the scale domain to the right by half the scale range.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.panLog
documentation:
Given a log scale domain array with numeric or datetime values, returns a new two-element
domain array that is the result of panning the domain by a fractional delta.

The delta value represents fractional units of the scale range; for example, 0.5 indicates
panning the scale domain to the right by half the scale range.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.panPow
documentation:
Given a power scale domain array with numeric or datetime values and the given exponent,
returns a new two-element domain array that is the result of panning the domain by a fractional delta.

The delta value represents fractional units of the scale range; for example, 0.5
indicates panning the scale domain to the right by half the scale range.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.panSymlog
documentation:
Given a symmetric log scale domain array with numeric or datetime values parameterized by the given
constant, returns a new two-element domain array that is the result of panning the domain by a fractional delta.

The delta value represents fractional units of the scale range; for example, 0.5
indicates panning the scale domain to the right by half the scale range.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.zoomLinear
documentation:
Given a linear scale domain array with numeric or datetime values, returns a new two-element domain
array that is the result of zooming the domain by a scaleFactor, centered at the provided fractional anchor.

The anchor value represents the zoom position in terms of fractional units of the scale range; for
example, 0.5 indicates a zoom centered on the mid-point of the scale range.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.zoomLog
documentation:
Given a log scale domain array with numeric or datetime values, returns a new two-element domain array
that is the result of zooming the domain by a scaleFactor, centered at the provided fractional anchor.

The anchor value represents the zoom position in terms of fractional units of the scale range; for
example, 0.5 indicates a zoom centered on the mid-point of the scale range.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.zoomPow
documentation:
Given a power scale domain array with numeric or datetime values and the given exponent, returns a new two-
element domain array that is the result of zooming the domain by a scaleFactor, centered at the provided fractional anchor.

The anchor value represents the zoom position in terms of fractional units of the scale range; for example, 0.5
indicates a zoom centered on the mid-point of the scale range.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.scale.zoomSymlog
documentation:
Given a symmetric log scale domain array with numeric or datetime values parameterized by the given constant,  returns a
new two- element domain array that is the result of zooming the domain by a scaleFactor, centered at the provided fractional
anchor.

The anchor value represents the zoom position in terms of fractional units of the scale range; for example, 0.5
indicates a zoom centered on the mid-point of the scale range.

modules.name: py2vega.functions.color
documentation: Color module that implements mocking Vega color functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.color.rgb
documentation:
Construct a new RGB color.

If r, g and b are specified, these represent the channel values of the returned color;
an opacity may also be specified. If a CSS Color Module Level 3 specifier string is
specified, it is parsed and then converted to the RGB color space. Uses d3-color’s rgb function.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.color.hsl
documentation:
Construct a new HSL color.

If h, s and l are specified, these represent the channel values of the returned color;
an opacity may also be specified. If a CSS Color Module Level 3 specifier string is
specified, it is parsed and then converted to the HSL color space. Uses d3-color’s hsl function.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.color.lab
documentation:
Construct a new CIE LAB color.

If l, a and b are specified, these represent the channel values of the returned color;
an opacity may also be specified. If a CSS Color Module Level 3 specifier string is
specified, it is parsed and then converted to the LAB color space. Uses d3-color’s lab function.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.color.hcl
documentation:
Construct a new HCL (hue, chroma, luminance) color.

If h, c and l are specified, these represent the channel values of the returned color;
an opacity may also be specified. If a CSS Color Module Level 3 specifier string is
specified, it is parsed and then converted to the HCL color space. Uses d3-color’s hcl function.

modules.name: py2vega.functions.regexp
documentation: Regexp module that implements mocking Vega regexp functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.regexp.regexp
documentation: Create a regular expression instance from an input pattern string and optional flags. Same as JavaScript’s RegExp.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.regexp.test
documentation:
Evaluate a regular expression regexp against the input string, returning true if the string matches the pattern, false otherwise.

For example: test(/\d{3}/, "32-21-9483") -> true.

modules.name: py2vega.functions.type_coercing
documentation: Module that implements mocking Vega type coercing functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_coercing.toBoolean
documentation: Coerce the input value to a string. None values and empty strings are mapped to None.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_coercing.toDate
documentation:
Coerce the input value to a Date instance. None values and empty strings are mapped to None.

If an optional parser function is provided, it is used to perform date parsing, otherwise Date.parse is used.
Be aware that Date.parse has different implementations across browsers!

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_coercing.toNumber
documentation: Coerce the input value to a number. None values and empty strings are mapped to None.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.type_coercing.toString
documentation: Coerce the input value to a string. None values and empty strings are mapped to None.

modules.name: py2vega.functions
documentation: noqa

modules.name: py2vega.functions.array
documentation: Module that implements mocking Vega array functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.extent
documentation:
Return a new [min, max] array with the minimum and maximum values of the input array,
ignoring null, undefined, and NaN values.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.clampRange
documentation:
Clamp a two-element range array in a span-preserving manner.

If the span of the input range is less than (max - min) and an endpoint exceeds either the min or max value, the
range is translated such that the span is preserved and one endpoint touches the boundary of the [min, max] range.
If the span exceeds (max - min), the range [min, max] is returned.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.indexof
documentation: Return the first index of value in the input array.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.inrange
documentation: Test whether value lies within (or is equal to either) the first and last values of the range array.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.join
documentation:
Return a new string by concatenating all of the elements of the input array,
separated by commas or a specified separator string.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.lastindexof
documentation: Return the last index of value in the input array.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.length
documentation: Return the length of the input array.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.lerp
documentation:
Return the linearly interpolated value between the first and last entries in the array for
the provided interpolation fraction (typically between 0 and 1). For example, lerp([0, 50], 0.5) returns 25.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.peek
documentation:
Return the last element in the input array.

Similar to the built-in Array.pop method, except that it does not remove the last element.
This method is a convenient shorthand for array[array.length - 1].

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.reverse
documentation:
Return a new array with elements in a reverse order of the input array.
The first array element becomes the last, and the last array element becomes the first.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.sequence
documentation:
Return an array containing an arithmetic sequence of numbers.

If step is omitted, it defaults to 1. If start is omitted, it defaults to 0. The stop value is exclusive;
it is not included in the result. If step is positive, the last element is the largest start + i * step less than stop; if
step is negative, the last element is the smallest start + i * step greater than stop. If the returned array would contain
an infinite number of values, an empty range is returned. The arguments are not required to be integers.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.slice
documentation:
Return a section of array between the start and end indices.

If the end argument is negative, it is treated as an offset
from the end of the array (length(array) + end).

functions.name: py2vega.functions.array.span
documentation: Return the span of array: the difference between the last and first elements, or array[array.length-1] - array[0].

modules.name: py2vega.functions.date_time
documentation: Module that implements mocking Vega date and time functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.now
documentation: Return the timestamp for the current time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.datetime
documentation: Return a new Date instance. The month is 0-based, such that 1 represents February.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.date
documentation: Return the day of the month for the given datetime value, in local time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.day
documentation: Return the day of the week for the given datetime value, in local time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.year
documentation: Return the year for the given datetime value, in local time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.quarter
documentation: Return the quarter of the year (0-3): for the given datetime value, in local time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.month
documentation: Return the (zero-based): month for the given datetime value, in local time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.hours
documentation: Return the hours component for the given datetime value, in local time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.minutes
documentation: Return the minutes component for the given datetime value, in local time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.seconds
documentation: Return the seconds component for the given datetime value, in local time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.milliseconds
documentation: Return the milliseconds component for the given datetime value, in local time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.time
documentation: Return the epoch-based timestamp for the given datetime value.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.timezoneoffset
documentation: Return the timezone offset from the local timezone to UTC for the given datetime value.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.utc
documentation: Return a timestamp for the given UTC date. The month is 0-based, such that 1 represents February.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.utcdate
documentation: Return the day of the month for the given datetime value, in UTC time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.utcday
documentation: Return the day of the week for the given datetime value, in UTC time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.utcyear
documentation: Return the year for the given datetime value, in UTC time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.utcquarter
documentation: Return the quarter of the year (0-3): for the given datetime value, in UTC time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.utcmonth
documentation: Return the (zero-based): month for the given datetime value, in UTC time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.utchours
documentation: Return the hours component for the given datetime value, in UTC time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.utcminutes
documentation: Return the minutes component for the given datetime value, in UTC time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.utcseconds
documentation: Return the seconds component for the given datetime value, in UTC time.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.date_time.utcmilliseconds
documentation: Return the milliseconds component for the given datetime value, in UTC time.

modules.name: py2vega.main
documentation: Python to VegaExpression transpiler.

functions.name: py2vega.main.validate
documentation:
Check whether or not a list of nodes is valid.

A list of nodes is considered valid when:
- it is not empty
- the last node is an `if` statement or a `return` statement
- everything but the last element is not an `if` statement or a `return` statement

functions.name: py2vega.main.valid_attribute_impl
documentation: Check the attribute access validity. Returns True if the member access is valid, False otherwise.

functions.name: py2vega.main.valid_attribute
documentation: Check the attribute access validity. Returns True if the member access is valid, False otherwise.

functions.name: py2vega.main.py2vega
documentation: Convert Python code or Python function to a valid Vega expression.

classes.name: py2vega.main.Variable

methods.name: py2vega.main.Variable.__init__
documentation: Construct a Variable, given its name and available members.
Helper class for defining a variable in whitelisting.

classes.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.generic_visit
documentation: Throwing an error by default.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Return
documentation: Turn a Python return statement into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_If
documentation: Turn a Python if statement into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Constant
documentation: Turn a Python Constant node into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor._visit_list_impl
documentation: Turn a Python list expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Tuple
documentation: Turn a Python tuple expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_List
documentation: Turn a Python list expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Dict
documentation: Turn a Python dict expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Assign
documentation: Turn a Python assignment expression into a Vega-expression. And save the assigned variable in the current scope.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_UnaryOp
documentation: Turn a Python unaryop expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_BoolOp
documentation: Turn a Python boolop expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_BinOp
documentation: Turn a Python binop expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_IfExp
documentation: Turn a Python if expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Compare
documentation: Turn a Python compare expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Name
documentation: Turn a Python name expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Call
documentation: Turn a Python call expression into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Subscript
documentation: Turn a Python Subscript node into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Index
documentation: Turn a Python subscript index into a Vega-expression.

methods.name: py2vega.main.VegaExpressionVisitor.visit_Attribute
documentation: Turn a Python attribute expression into a Vega-expression.
Visitor that turns a Node into a Vega expression.

modules.name: py2vega.functions.math
documentation: Math module that implements mocking Vega mathematical functions.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.isNaN
documentation: Return true if value is not a number. Same as JavaScript’s isNaN.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.isFinite
documentation: Return true if value is a finite number. Same as JavaScript’s isFinite.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.abs
documentation: Returns the absolute value of value. Same as JavaScript’s Math.abs.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.acos
documentation: Trigonometric arccosine. Same as JavaScript’s Math.acos.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.asin
documentation: Trigonometric arcsine. Same as JavaScript’s Math.asin.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.atan
documentation: Trigonometric arctangent. Same as JavaScript’s Math.atan.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.atan2
documentation: Returns the arctangent of dy / dx. Same as JavaScript’s Math.atan2.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.ceil
documentation: Rounds value to the nearest integer of equal or greater value. Same as JavaScript’s Math.ceil.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.clamp
documentation: Restricts value to be between the specified min and max.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.cos
documentation: Trigonometric cosine. Same as JavaScript’s Math.cos.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.exp
documentation: Returns the value of e raised to the provided exponent. Same as JavaScript’s Math.exp.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.floor
documentation: Rounds value to the nearest integer of equal or lower value. Same as JavaScript’s Math.floor.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.log
documentation: Returns the natural logarithm of value. Same as JavaScript’s Math.log.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.max
documentation: Returns the maximum argument value. Same as JavaScript’s Math.max.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.min
documentation: Returns the minimum argument value. Same as JavaScript’s Math.min.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.pow
documentation: Returns value raised to the given exponent. Same as JavaScript’s Math.pow.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.random
documentation: Returns a pseudo-random number in the range [0,1). Same as JavaScript’s Math.random.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.round
documentation: Rounds value to the nearest integer. Same as JavaScript’s Math.round.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.sin
documentation: Trigonometric sine. Same as JavaScript’s Math.sin.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.sqrt
documentation: Square root function. Same as JavaScript’s Math.sqrt.

functions.name: py2vega.functions.math.tan
documentation: Trigonometric tangent. Same as JavaScript’s Math.tan.
